seo.title: Building Hybrid keyword and vector search with Algolia an...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Hybrid keyword and vector search with Algolia and Typesense.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to implementing an AI-powered search system, focusing on hybrid keyword-vector search, reranking, and UI integration. Steps address latency, indexing, and ranking challenges specific to AI search workflows.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of search query DSL

steps.title: Set up search infrastructure
steps.explanation: Provision an Elasticsearch cluster with vector support or configure Pinecone with OpenAI embeddings. Install required plugins like elasticsearch-knn for vector search capabilities.
pitfalls: Missing vector plugin causes index failures
Incompatible Elasticsearch version with vector extensions

steps.title: Create hybrid search index
steps.explanation: Design an index mapping that combines keyword fields (text) with vector fields (embeddings). Use a multi-field approach to enable both term matching and semantic similarity searches.
pitfalls: Incorrect vector dimensions cause indexing errors
Missing keyword fields limit traditional search capabilities

steps.title: Implement hybrid search query
steps.explanation: Construct a search query that combines match_all with a knn_search for vector similarity. Use function_score to weight results by both keyword relevance and vector distance.
pitfalls: Improper score_mode leads to unexpected ranking
KNN search without filtering causes excessive computation

steps.title: Add reranking with business logic
steps.explanation: Use a separate API (e.g., Cohere Rerank) to reorder results based on custom criteria after initial vector search. Pass original query and results to the reranker for context-aware sorting.

code.content:
import cohere
co = cohere.Client('YOUR_API_KEY')
response = co.rerank(
  query='search term',
  documents=[result['text'] for result in initial_results],
  top_n=5
)

pitfalls: Reranking API rate limits during high traffic
Missing document context causes poor reranking decisions

steps.title: Build search UI components
steps.explanation: Create a search bar with auto-suggestions, result cards showing confidence scores, and filters for different content types. Implement loading states for asynchronous search requests.
pitfalls: Ignoring loading states causes UI freezes
Missing confidence indicators leads to unclear result quality

content.conclusion: This implementation addresses core AI search challenges through hybrid indexing, reranking, and structured UI components. Validate each stage with real user queries and monitor performance metrics for continuous optimization.
